pairs.question: What is the title of the third episode of the third season of The X-Files?
pairs.answer: The title of the third episode of the third season of The X-Files is 'D.P.O.'.

pairs.question: When did the episode 'D.P.O.' first air in the United States?
pairs.answer: 'D.P.O.' first aired in the United States on October 6, 1995, on Fox.

pairs.question: Who wrote and directed the episode 'D.P.O.'?
pairs.answer: The episode 'D.P.O.' was written by Howard Gordon and directed by Kim Manners.

pairs.question: What is the premise of the episode 'D.P.O.'?
pairs.answer: In 'D.P.O.', FBI special agents Fox Mulder and Dana Scully investigate a series of lightning-related deaths in Oklahoma, which are linked to a youth named Darin who survived multiple lightning strikes that killed five other men.

pairs.question: How did the creators develop the concept for 'D.P.O.'?
pairs.answer: The original concept for 'D.P.O.' was a one-line idea titled 'Lightning Boy,' which had been pinned to a board in series creator Chris Carter's office since the first season.

pairs.question: What notable special effect was used in 'D.P.O.' to depict lightning strikes?
pairs.answer: A 'lightning machine' created by Special Effects Supervisor David Gauthier was buried underground and used to simulate the lightning strike sequence involving the character Darin.

pairs.question: What Nielsen rating did 'D.P.O.' achieve during its initial broadcast?
pairs.answer: 'D.P.O.' earned a Nielsen household rating of 10.9 and was watched by 15.57 million people in its initial broadcast.
